The document is riddled with mixed languages (English/French glosses in parentheses), erratic punctuation, dangling English words, and a randomly injected URL. These critical errors violate basic professional translation standards and render the text unpolished and difficult to read as a cohesive piece. While the underlying Spanish terminology is generally acceptable, the presentation quality is critically low. The translation requires a complete clean-up, removal of all mixed-language annotations, correction of punctuation, and elimination of the URL to meet any standard of quality.
